Static rifts and planar waves

Wiki

HomeGetting StartedAdventurer LevelsPlanar RiftsBreach GatesWorld BossesCreaturesLoot and RelicsShrines and LoreBounty BoardsReference

Planar Rifts

Standard rifts are static world anchors. Players travel to them, activate them, and face enemies that spill into the world in waves.

How Rifts Work

  1. A player finds or receives an Ancient Map Scroll.
  2. The player travels to a static rift anchor.
  3. Activation opens a portal above the rift crystal.
  4. Enemies emerge one or two at a time for a short period.
  5. The portal closes after the encounter finishes.

Anchor Themes

Rifts are placed in locations that match their creature theme. Undead rifts belong in crypts and ruins, infernal rifts near scorched or corrupted spaces, beast rifts in wilderness, and sewer rifts in the starter sewer route.

AnchorThemeTypical Use
Undead LordSkeletons, liches, death magicCrypts, graveyards, Ruin.
Sewer VerminRats and starter sewer threatsEarly rift introduction.
Black Forest HuntPredators and wild huntersForest danger areas.
Barren WastesScorpions and desert threatsBarren Lands.
Corruption VoidVoid-touched armiesCorruption dungeon route.

Rewards

Rift enemies are stronger than normal creatures in the same area and can carry improved loot, Adventurer XP, map scroll drops, and rare relic chances.
